programme.desc: On the island of Madagascar, paleontologists discover an intact fossil unlike any known animal, living or extinct; bizarre stone spheres are discovered in Costa Rica; the curious remains of a small man are found in Chiapas, Mexico.

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: Security cameras in China capture a sudden explosion beneath a city street that buries two young girls in debris; experts investigate and uncover a black market of secondhand cooking oil that may be dangerously festering in the city's sewers.

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: A dashcam captures an explosion that blows out the backseat and windows of a car on a Russian highway; experts investigate and uncover the vehicle's potentially disastrous performance modifications.

programme.title: Strange Evidence
programme.desc: A surveillance camera captures a woman vanishing from her bed as she sleeps next to her husband in their suburban English home; experts investigate and uncover numerous police records that report a strange pattern of nearby alien abductions.

programme.desc: Cameras in Kazakhstan capture a bizarre blast as train cars on a quiet railway suddenly explode in a raging inferno.

programme.title: Strange Evidence

programme.title: Killers of the Cosmos
programme.desc: More than 100 million pieces of small space debris are floating around the Earth; a collision between two of these objects in low Earth orbit could be catastrophic.

programme.title: NASA's Unexplained Files
programme.desc: When a former Area 51 insider alleges that the Roswell UFO incident was a nefarious Soviet conspiracy masterminded by Stalin, experts investigate if it was indeed an enemy plot against America -- or if the confession is just another cover-up.

programme.title: NASA's Unexplained Files
programme.desc: Neil Armstrong enters an unexplained spin; an Apollo 17 photo seems to show a US military outpost on the Moon; possible alien life swimming in the oceans of Jupiter's moons.

programme.title: How the Universe Works
programme.desc: Earth's history is marked by one cosmic disaster after another, forging the path of human evolution; now, experts explore how the Earth has teetered on the verge of destruction.

programme.title: How the Universe Works
programme.desc: As the two Voyager spacecraft reach interstellar space, experts explore their astonishing discoveries and how their extended operation has transformed into the ultimate mission.

programme.title: Super Factories
programme.desc: The largest steel plant in the world is a high-tech super factory that takes production to astonishing levels, turning out 21 million tons of steel every year on a sprawling campus comprised of 50 individually operating subfactories.

programme.title: Super Factories
programme.desc: NASA's hi-tech rocket assembly facility pushes the limits of space exploration to new frontiers; here, engineers build an advanced rocket propulsion system that can send astronauts and cargo to the moon and back.

programme.title: Super Factories
programme.desc: McLaren's high-tech production center is as remarkably distinctive as the handcrafted automobiles it designs and produces, overtaking its supercar rivals through the application of Formula One science and sophisticated production standards.

programme.title: Super Factories
programme.desc: Legendary automotive brand Rolls-Royce is also one of the largest manufacturers of aircraft engines and produces the revolutionary Trent XWB, one of the world's most powerful, efficient and eco-friendly jet engines.

programme.title: Super Factories
programme.desc: A state-of-the-art Volkswagen factory uses cutting-edge robot technology to produce 750 vehicles every day, and special behind-the-scenes access to the factory floor reveal how this facility is one of the most advanced in the world.

programme.title: Super Factories
programme.desc: The UPS Worldport is a distribution powerhouse that connects the world and special access reveals what happens in the largest automated sorting hub on the planet; with its own fleet of 550 aircraft, it serves more destinations than any other airline.

programme.title: Super Factories: Food Edition
programme.desc: Special access to cutting-edge factories reveals how America's favorite foods are made and how assembly lines produce huge amounts of hot dogs, chocolate bars and other treats.

programme.title: Engineering Catastrophes
programme.desc: In Puerto Rico, an iconic observatory nestled into a giant crater has to close after cables holding up a critical platform come crashing down; experts analyze the cumulative effects that led to the collapse of this awe-inspiring structure.

programme.title: Engineering Catastrophes
programme.desc: Tragedy strikes the heart of a city as an earthquake wipes out a building full of people in Christchurch, New Zealand; experts investigate an inadequate structural design and weakness in critical supporting columns.

programme.title: Engineering Catastrophes
programme.desc: A crane collapse leaves seven dead on a holiday weekend in Manhattan; an investigation reveals how a shortcut taken while raising the crane left the machine swaying in the wind without proper lateral support.

programme.title: Engineering Catastrophes

programme.title: Struggle Meals

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: Frankie prepares a Valentine's Day dinner for under $15 with a bistro-style theme to up the romance. Why go to a crowded restaurant for an overpriced prix-fixe menu with bad service when you can prepare a simple yet decadent romantic meal at home.

programme.title: Struggle Meals
programme.desc: Dried beans are affordable and healthy to prepare. Frankie will teach the basics of preparing beans from scratch while making tasty recipes. We also take a field trip to the grocery store to shop the bulk aisle. They're also good for your heart.

programme.title: Struggle Meals

programme.title: Struggle Meals

programme.desc: José Leôncio, hacendado del Pantanal, deja a una joven embarazada en una visita a Río de Janeiro. Ella va a vivir con él al Pantanal, pero es infeliz y decide volver a la ciudad con su hijo. Veinte años después, este va en busca de su padre.

programme.desc: Lynn's Table gets a bad review; the Action News Team must cater to Chandler's demands after he buys new equipment.

programme.title: The Synanon Fix: Did the Cure Become a Cult?
programme.desc: Recovering alcoholic Charles Dederich opens a drug rehabilitation program; Synanon, as he names it, soon attracts celebrities and non-addicts who donate their time and wealth; the community grows into a movement that expands across the country.

programme.title: The Synanon Fix: Did the Cure Become a Cult?
programme.desc: Chuck establishes "The Synanon Religion" and maintains their tax-exempt status; Chuck becomes increasingly authoritative and dictatorial; many members find themselves weighing the benefits and costs of the choices they're forced to make.

programme.title: The Synanon Fix: Did the Cure Become a Cult?
programme.desc: Chuck's behavior becomes more belligerent and abusive; he forms a military-like boot camp for delinquent children where corporal punishment abounds; TIME Magazine publishes "Swinging at Synanon" and Chuck sues for $76 million as his paranoia grows.

programme.title: The Synanon Fix: Did the Cure Become a Cult?
programme.desc: In 1977, the Los Angeles Times reports on the nine-day abduction of Frances Winn by Synanon; Chuck spends $62,000 on guns and ammunition; sentenced to five years probation, Chuck loses everything and dies in a nursing home.

programme.title: If I Had Legs I'd Kick You

programme.title: The Fate of the Furious
programme.desc: With Dom and Letty married, Brian and Mia retired and the rest of the crew exonerated, the globe-trotting team has found some semblance of a normal life. They soon face an unexpected challenge when a woman named Cipher forces Dom to betray them all. They must unite to bring home the man who made them a family and stop Cipher from unleashing chaos.

programme.title: Friendship

But just when it seems the lovers will soon be reunited, they each score a big break that could separate them for good.

programme.title: I Give It a Year

programme.title: Girls Trip

programme.title: The Yogurt Shop Murders

programme.title: The Yogurt Shop Murders
programme.desc: As the investigation increasingly focuses on the young suspects, police subject them to further videotaped interrogations, which reveal contradictory and unstable versions of what happened and some confessions.

programme.title: The Yogurt Shop Murders
programme.desc: When new DNA evidence leads to Springsteen and Scott's release, the victims' families must grapple with the unknown.

programme.title: Bring Her Back

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Road Trip
programme.desc: Gino & Fred are excited to head to San Francisco for their very own Summer of Love. Gordon tries to embrace the flower power mood, but he's more interested in the 61 Michelin Stars. The boys travel to a Napa Valley vineyard in a hot air balloon.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Road Trip
programme.desc: After four weeks on the road Gino's airing his feet in the back seat as the guys cruise along a Texan highway. Texas is a meat lovers paradise and Gordon is keen to show the Europhiles some of the best BBQ known to man.

programme.title: World's Most Incredible Hotels
programme.desc: In Australia, in the popular city of Melbourne are two hotels that attract visitors from around the world. In the heart of the city is the oldest heritage hotel in Australia. And an hour's drive South of the city is the vineyard hotel The Jackalope.

programme.title: World's Most Incredible Hotels
programme.desc: We visit two hotels in Eastern Europe which although separated by miles, share the same values of combining their ancient heritage with modern and ground-breaking architecture to deliver their guests a one-of-a-kind experience.

programme.title: World's Most Incredible Hotels
programme.desc: In this episode, we visit Italy - the birthplace of western civilization and home to two incredible hotels set against some of this country's most stunning backdrops which embody the very best of their own regional history and culture.

programme.title: World's Most Incredible Hotels
programme.desc: We visit two hotels that have utilized their desert location in the United Arab Emirates to offer guests unique stays found in a few places in the world.

programme.title: World's Most Incredible Hotels
programme.desc: In Thailand, are the Anantara Golden Triangle and Elephant Camp Resort. Attracting guests who desire luxury and adventure. In the Philippines lies Villa Escudero Plantation and Resort. This picturesque and tranquil Villa is brimming with character.

programme.title: World's Most Incredible Hotels
programme.desc: We visit the vast continent of the USA. From the hustle and bustle of the east to the relaxed, atmospheric west, two contrasting hotels offer an American adventure with a difference.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Road Trip
programme.desc: Gordon, Gino and Fred start their road trip in Gino's homeland of Italy, where Gino needs help to pull off an event that is close to his heart. Gino collects Gordon and Fred in a campervan to begin a road trip they won't be forgetting in a hurry.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Road Trip
programme.desc: Gordon, Gino and Fred travel to France, where Fred has been asked to co-host an Oyster Festival in the beautiful Arcachon Bay, in just four days' time. Reunited with their campervan, Fred nominates himself as the driver in his homeland.

programme.title: Europe's Greatest Train Journeys
programme.desc: Andalusia, now crossed by high-speed and modernized rail lines, unveils its rich culture and landscapes; along the journey, we visit cities like Seville, Granada with its famed Alhambra and Guadix near the Tabernas desert.

programme.title: Europe's Greatest Train Journeys
programme.desc: In Portugal, in the Douro River Valley, a steam locomotive and a small historic train will take you in turn to the water's edge, on the only railroad line in the world that crosses three World Heritage Sites classified by UNESCO.

programme.title: Europe's Greatest Train Journeys
programme.desc: The Little Yellow Train winds through the Eastern Pyrenees, crossing the Cerdan plateau, the historic Gisclard railway suspension bridge and the Grandes Canalettes caves, known as the underground Versailles of the Pyrenees.

programme.title: Europe's Greatest Train Journeys
programme.desc: The Cinque Terre Express runs along Italy's northern coast east of Genoa; passing through five UNESCO-listed villages, it offers coastal views and access to cobbled streets, harbors, terraced vineyards and pastel-colored houses.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's India
programme.desc: Luke heads to the city of Bangalore in southern India; he visits MTR, one of the oldest coffee houses in the city; he learns how to make idli; he meets up with a friend to explore the night markets.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's India
programme.desc: Luke learns about Kongunadu cuisine in Coimbatore, partners with a family in Thrissur to create an authentic delicacy and learns about India's rich coffee culture.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's India
programme.desc: Luke visits the port city of Kochi, Kerala, where he checks out the iconic Chinese fishing nets, does laundry the old-fashioned way and cooks a famous dish. He leaves the city to witness the state's backwaters and local fishing techniques.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's India
programme.desc: Luke visits the lush plantations of spices in Thekkady and then tours Madurai, the spiritual heart of Tamil Nadu, to find hidden gems with the help of a local food journalist.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's India
programme.desc: Luke uncovers the history of Chettinad and savours its famous cuisine. He then tastes the chutneys in Kumbakonam and explores the exciting food scene in Pondicherry.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's India
programme.desc: Luke visits Chennai, the capital of Tamil Nadu, a bustling city with historic ancient temples, and a vibrant modern art scene.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Railway Vietnam
programme.desc: Luke searches to find Ho Chi Minh's old markets before they disappear due to District 1's rapid development. Join Luke as he gathers fresh ingredients from a local market, tries some traditional delicacies, and follows his sweet tooth to Marou.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Railway Vietnam
programme.desc: Luke's next stop is the destination of Dalat. Follow Luke as he visits the Art Deco Dalat Train Station, experiences the French influence of the region with a Father and Son chef duo, and discovers why Dalat is known as the'Land Of Eternal Spring!

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Railway Vietnam
programme.desc: Luke hops on the train in Ga Thap Cham, and finds himself in Nha Trang- a region of Vietnam with a similar vibe to the French Riviera! Luke hand-catches seafood, looks for some fresh durian fruit, and visits a barramundi farm to harvest sea grapes!

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Railway Vietnam
programme.desc: Luke is in the town of Hoi An. He visits a family who has been making chilli sauce for 5 generations, stops by the Historic Japanese Covered Bridge built in 1719, and meets a 4th generation blacksmith who is creating knives from bomb shells.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Railway Vietnam
programme.desc: On this stop of his Vietnam journey, Luke visits the bustling seaside city of Da Nang. Join him as he visits the spectacular Dragon Bridge, looks up some succulent shimp on the beach, and stumbles upon a tiny fishing village packed with restaurants.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Railway Vietnam
programme.desc: Luke visits Huê- famous for its heritage and cuisine and Con Hen Village to learn a technique for de-shelling baby clams, searches for best bowl of spicy beef noodle soup, and stops by a business where they they make turmeric vermicelli noodle salad!

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Railway Vietnam
programme.desc: After a long trip from Hue, Luke finally gets some rest in the scenic Ninh Bihn. Tag along as Luke meets with a local rice farmer, takes a canoe out for some crab hunting, and ventures deep into the jungle in search of a local delicacy- red ant eggs!

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Railway Vietnam
programme.desc: Luke is in northern Vietnam- Hai Phong! This is the birthplace of his partner Lynne. Join Luke as he meets some of Lynne's family for the first time, and tries the delcious Banh Da Noodle- a tasty dish that Hai Phong is renowned for!

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Railway Vietnam
programme.desc: Luke's penultimate stop on his train journey is Hanoi. Follow Luke on his hunt for an iconic Hanoi dish, Bun Cha. Luke takes a ride on the Reunification Express railway, and visits the famous Hoan Kiem Lake for some delcious magenta sticky rice.

programme.title: Luke Nguyen's Railway Vietnam
programme.desc: Nearing the end of his Vitenamese journey, Luke stops in Sapa, a hilltop region that he remembers from his travels fifteen years ago. Join Luke as he rekindles a friendship from his previous visit and learns about a local dish of dried buffalo.

programme.title: Extraordinary Places to Eat
programme.desc: TV and radio host Dermot O'Leary takes Fred Sirieix to Puglia in Italy's deep South to his favourite restaurants showcasing simple but sublime cucina povera or poor man's cooking.

programme.title: Extraordinary Places to Eat
programme.desc: Restaurateur Thomasina Miers takes Fred to South Wales to explore some wild places to eat from an ancient inn to a beachside restaurant and wood fired cooking.

programme.title: Extraordinary Places to Eat
programme.desc: Fred travels to South Devon with chef and restauranteur Angela Hartnett. From Angela's all-time favourite restaurant in Dartmouth to an incredible floating cafe on the River Exe.

programme.title: Extraordinary Places to Eat
programme.desc: Pastry Chef Ravneet Gill takes Fred to Mallorca to experience incredible island cooking, from a buzzing beach bar serving holiday food in the sun, to light and fluffy pastries.

programme.title: Extraordinary Places to Eat
programme.desc: Chef Giorgio Locatelli takes Fred to Rome in search of the city's most exciting eats. Sublime pasta, fluffy gnocchi and the world's best pizza all delicious but affordable.

programme.title: Extraordinary Places to Eat
programme.desc: Acclaimed chef Michael Caines takes Fred to Burgundy to amazing places to eat serving the finest French country cooking and exquisite patisserie.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver Cooks the Mediterranean

programme.title: Jamie Oliver Cooks the Mediterranean
programme.desc: Jamie's soaking up inspiration in Spain with tapas, flavor-charged rice dishes, and spectacular seafood. He cooks a stunning pork chop with peppers and alioli and a surf and turf skewer of prawns, mushrooms, and local ham.
